reviews.review_title: A Space Combat Game Made For VR
reviews.review_description:
I got to say, if you give up easy at difficult controls, or hard to learn games, then don't buy it. But if you like to learn and you have a knaxk for dealing with learning curves, this is a good game. Like it's a little wonky and it takes several missions to get a hang of it and I'm still getting the hang of it, even the voice controls but once I'm starting to figure it out, it's getting more and more fun. 

I mean VR and the way this space simulator works is a match made in heaven. You really feel like you're watching these ships and you're kind of floating in outer space. And the fact that it's procedurally generated, as far as it told me, that's awesome for replayability. I'm starting out on easy which I never do because of the controls, and now that I'm getting The knack of voice commands, that's a huge game changer and makes it really fun. 

If you like sci-fi at all or any of the Star wars or Star Trek type of stuff, you're really going to like this game especially if you're a fan of real-time strategy. 

Just do it.

it can't hold a candle to the best RTS games.
it's a nice show of holographs VR provides, but that doesn't add much, if anything, to RTS. it's like playing chess in real life, versus 2D chess, single player or online. a 3D view makes little/no difference for strategy gaming.

developer_response.body: Hi BoyFly, there is no narration for the tutorial.

reviews.review_description: Aint got played 10 min worst control ever but no refund possible

reviews.review_title: Top rated game for sure .... BUT
reviews.review_description: This is definitely one of the most top-rated games I have ever played in my life I mean let's look at the facts It has every single thing I could possibly want in a video game from open space combat to being able to see the map systems on how wide of a range or attack is the game is 100% everything I would have ever wanted in a VR gaming system.... BUT I know you guys have joystick movements in the game but I don't like to use that setting because it takes away from the rotating screen setting It would be cool if at some point in time you guys drop some kind of update making that whole system with the rotation a lot more smoother because sometimes when I'm mid-game in the middle of a fight and try to rotate the screen I will fly all the way off map and have to pause the game and shorten the screen just to get all the way back

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback about the grip rotation - we can look into that. One trick btw, if you find yourself far away from the battle, you can look at your left hand ship menu and click on a ship to teleport to it instantly. With voice you can also say "Show flagship".

reviews.review_description: It’s a great game and with the added endless mode you get to continue the fun for a bunch more missions.

This is the game I've wanted to play since I was a kid in the 1980's...not just moving ships around on a screen, but grabbing them and assigning orders in 3D space.  It's a whole new immersive dynamic, like Homeworld on steroids.  

Once I got the hang of the controls, I could focus totally on strategy and tactics. Unlike other space fleet battle sims, three-dimensional thinking is possible, with ships plunging down on enemies from above.  

The ship weapons are fantastic, and even though I just finished the story mode, I'm looking forward to going back and trying other  strategies and tactics in the story and skirmish modes.

All aspects of the game are well polished, and Eternal Starlight sets a high bar for VR fleet battle sims to follow!

reviews.review_title: My Favorite Quest Game - By Far
reviews.review_description:
I finally found the game that keeps me on my meta quest 3. It is challenging to play, but fair, has several great game modes, has a great backstory that allows the player to slowly build up skills and understanding of the game and ultimately to use those skills to beat the harder levels. It is very simple interface to use with both hand, controller, and voice commands available. The AI for the enemy ships is extremely well done, making it all the more challenging to win. the game is very configurable too, to make the gameplay easier or harder, depending on what you’re after. The 
easier settings are easier to work all the way through the missions, but the harder settings allow you to have a higher score if that’s what you’re after. But with some time and practice, this game can be able to be mastered.

Absolutely great game that I’d highly recommend!

This review has been edited to reflect the developer's explanation of issues I had with scoring: First, let me say that I love this game. This is one of my most played games on the quest. The gameplay is awesome, the graphics and lighting are great and the overall ambiance is fantastic. Another great thing about this game is the replayability. You can try and get the high score in endless mode and you can also try to achieve the most wins in story mode. The developers of the game are very involved and regularly make updates and changes to keep the game interesting.
It's impossible to get around on this game .it's just a horrible game I bought it cause I seen great reviews but it sucks so not buy this game

reviews.review_title: I don't know how to feel
reviews.review_description:
Wow...normally I would say that a short game doesn't matter but this is the exception. Maybe the value of $20 has changed, I don't know. I get that I was playing on easy mode but it got super boring super fast.

That's a flaw within itself and I bought this because a VR RTS are rare. It's not awful but that's within the perspective of VR( whose standards are going down ). The scale is great, the controls work... you could honestly do it 1 handed they're so simple.

It's weird. Going on record that the only VR RTS that I know of is Out of Bullets. It had specialized units, specialized enemies, and high risks. This felt like shovelware

Obvious Problem: you have to complete the campaign for skirmish... that's down right annoying. Nothing is nicer than paying $20 just to say "You can't play this mode without completing this mode first." I understand why you would do this for multiplayer because why let someone who doesn't know what they're doing play against those who do.
I enjoy it, but it's a little to gimmicky for the $20 price tag. This was not 1/3 of a AAA game.

I bought this game because it was discounted and by glowing reviews. After installing, I tried the tutorial and it stopped working and returned to space station and menu. Trying to move around is not like most Oculus Quest 2 games work. 
This is not an intuitive game or easy to figure out.  It I don’t understand how it works after a few tries, I uninstall it. Waste of money.


Update: I did check joystick preference but it is no familiar transport icon like other Oculus apps. I still have issues with the tutorials returning to main room after few moves trying to learn the game. I will it few more attempts but still leaving me with bad impression of game . It does have nice vr graphics and concept is one of reason I bought the game but waste of money if it is frustrating to play.

developer_response.body: Hi, we're sorry to hear that your first experience wasn't great. If you decide to give it another try, I'd recommend turning on the Joystick movement in the settings page - that may be more familiar to you. The game uses the controller grips to move and zoom in/out. Also, if you've played less than two hours you can get a refund, if it's not your cup of tea.

reviews.review_description:
The games a wonderful idea, but you failed to worn consumer that “If you die” you start from the beginning with nothing. Once you have already purchased the game it’s to late to get your money back. Maybe you should put that in the “overview” so other people won’t have to make the same mistake I did. How can you enjoy a game that doesn’t allow you too keep what you have earned.
This game is a “Phish” In my opinion.
There has to be a law against not making such a thing public too your consumer base. Think 

I’m going to look into it, that’s pretty @&)# up!!

Respectfully 
Mr Palm

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry to hear that - the description says it's a roguelike which typically means it has permadeath. The first screen when you start a new game also says this - if you've played less than 2 hours, you can still get a refund.

reviews.review_description: This game sucks

reviews.review_title: The best strategy game on Oculus
reviews.review_description:
So far been really impressed with the replayability of this game. You would think it would be awful dying and starting back over at the very beginning every time I mean completely back over at the beginning. But it allows you to try different strategies for each time you go through it. The graphics and the controls are top-notch it didn't seem very intuitive at the time but once you get playing with a minute or so it's comes easy. 
The only thing I would ask the developers to change is that since you do have to start back over at the beginning each time why do you have to click through the same speeches from the computer avatars that gets to be a quite redundant. Or I just haven't found the skip button
Love this game! Needs more missions!

Noticed a couple reviews complain about not being able to turn. The solution is to enable joystick turning in the settings, not sure why someone would write a review without looking through the options menu first. 10/10 game, adds some much needed variety to the oculus store as well.

This game is fun, but the permadeath is frustrating. spending hours building up a ship just to have it all gone in an instant and restart from scratch.
I hope to see more game options added to balance things out. Like retry level and permadeath as an option not mandatory. Also store always available would be nice. 
there are a few economy balance issues, even on normal mode you must be very strategic with your cash and sometimes will make no cash on a mission whilst still needing repairs.

Most of these critiques will be addressed I'm sure as this game has been out less than a month as of writing of this review. 
This is a solid space strategy game. If they were going for an "enders game" feel they nailed it.
This soryline is so short I finished the game in 1.5 hours. and thats with taking a 30 minute break. The game is fun but needs way more content for it to even be worth $20

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback. We've heard from a lot of people that they'd like more content.
Here's a page listing what's coming in version 1.3, which will add a new campaign: https://www.starlight-vr.com/pages/endless.html

reviews.review_description: This game is for nerds the simulation is easy to control I like that you can mod and upgrade your fleet but I think the graphics should be updated a bit more.
